skill.name: subagent-driven-development
skill.description: Coordinates subagent-based execution of implementation plans with implementation, spec compliance review, and code quality review in one session.
skill.summary: Run implementation plans through implementer and reviewer subagents.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 38 potential issues, but review found no malicious code, prompt injection, network calls, or executable scripts. Most findings are false positives from Markdown fences, prompt placeholders, and git SHA labels. The skill remains medium risk because it coordinates subagents that can modify files, run tests, inspect code, and commit changes in a repository.

medium_findings.title: Agentic Repository Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill directs implementer subagents to implement tasks, write tests, verify changes, and commit work. This is expected for a development workflow, but it can change files and invoke project tooling in the active repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited prompt text clearly asks subagents to perform implementation, testing, and commits. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it creates repository modification risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Flagged as Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: Static external command findings are false positives. The cited sections are Markdown fenced examples and prompt templates, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters are Markdown code fences around documentation examples. No executable script, command runner, or interpolation path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain task labels, headings, ordinary prose, or git SHA placeholders. They do not define cryptographic algorithms or security logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic implementation. The only SHA-related text is a git commit reference placeholder.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Review Checklist Text
low_findings.description: Static reconnaissance findings are false positives. The cited lines ask subagents to compare requirements, inspect code, and validate claims; they do not request system or network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording is a code review checklist. No commands, endpoints, host discovery, port scanning, or credential discovery instructions were found.

low_findings.title: Illustrative Hidden Config Path
low_findings.description: Line 107 is an example answer choosing a user-level hook path under ~/.config. It is not a command or automated file access, but it signals possible filesystem interaction when applied to real tasks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plainly part of a documentation example, not active code. The path is still relevant because the workflow may ask subagents to edit user-level configuration during real tasks.

content.value_statement: Large implementation plans are hard to execute consistently across many tasks. This skill coordinates implementer, spec reviewer, and code reviewer subagents with review loops.
actual_capabilities: Breaks an existing implementation plan into task-level subagent work.
Provides prompt templates for implementer, spec reviewer, and code quality reviewer roles.
Defines a two-stage review loop for spec compliance and code quality.
Guides subagents to ask clarifying questions before and during implementation.
Encourages testing, self-review, and commits for each completed task.
limitations: Requires a clear implementation plan before use.
Works best when tasks are mostly independent.
Does not provide executable automation or project-specific commands.
Adds cost and time through multiple subagent review passes.

use_cases.title: Execute a Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to complete a multi-task feature plan with implementation and review after each task.

use_cases.title: Improve Review Discipline
use_cases.description: Use separate reviewer prompts to check that code matches the requested specification before judging code quality.

use_cases.title: Coordinate AI Coding Sessions
use_cases.description: Use a controller session to keep task context organized while fresh subagents handle focused implementation work.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with one independent task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development for this plan. Start with Task 1, provide the full task text to an implementer subagent, and ask for questions before coding.

prompt_templates.title: Run Review Gates
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use after implementation is reported complete.
prompt_templates.prompt: After the implementer reports back, dispatch the spec reviewer first. If it passes, dispatch the code quality reviewer with the task summary and commit range.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Reviewer Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use when a reviewer finds gaps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Send the reviewer findings back to the implementer subagent. Ask it to fix only those issues, rerun relevant tests, and report the updated result.

prompt_templates.title: Complete a Multi-Task Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use across a full implementation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract every task from the plan, create a task list, run implementer and two reviewer passes per task, then request a final full implementation review.

output_examples.input: Execute a five-task feature plan in the current repository.
output_examples.output: A task list with each item implemented, checked against the specification, reviewed for code quality, tested, and marked complete only after review approval.

output_examples.input: The spec reviewer says a required progress report is missing.
output_examples.output: The implementer receives the exact issue, adds the missing behavior, reruns relevant tests, and sends the change back for another spec review.
A final reviewer checks the complete implementation before branch completion steps begin.

best_practices: Provide each subagent with the full task text and only the context it needs.
Run spec compliance review before code quality review for every task.
Answer subagent questions before allowing implementation to continue.
anti_patterns: Do not use this workflow without a written implementation plan.
Do not skip reviewer rechecks after fixes.
Do not run multiple implementation subagents on conflicting files at the same time.

faq.question: Does this skill write code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and prompt templates for coordinating coding subagents.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when you have a clear implementation plan with tasks that can be handled mostly independently.

faq.question: Why are there two reviewer roles?
faq.answer: The first reviewer checks whether the work matches the specification. The second reviewer checks maintainability, tests, and code quality.

faq.question: Can it run tasks in parallel?
faq.answer: The skill recommends one implementation subagent at a time to avoid conflicting changes.

faq.question: Does it require Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: It is written for agentic coding tools such as Claude, Codex, and Claude Code that can dispatch subagents.

faq.question: What is the main safety concern?
faq.answer: Subagents may edit files, run tests, and commit changes, so users should review changes before merging.
